summary: Completed backend + partial frontend testing of the massive update (8 modules). Backend: 20/20 pytest cases pass after fixing a critical Pydantic model bug (Client.lat/lng missing fields). Frontend: Dashboard, /tour, /reglages, /clients-status render; all Settings goal/brand testids present; clients/status route ordering is correct and returns array (not 404).

critical.issue: FIXED by testing agent: raised 500 whenever an address was provided. Root cause: `Client` Pydantic model had no lat/lng fields but `clients_create` was doing `c.lat, c.lng = await auto_geocode(...)`, raising `ValueError: "Client" object has no field "lat"`. Added `lat: Optional[float] = None` and `lng: Optional[float] = None` to the Client model.

ui_bugs.issue: 'RDV À VENIR' KPI displays '()' instead of the upcoming count. Likely a template rendering `({count})` with an empty value.
Missing `data-testid="clients-status-page"` on the page root (review request expects it). Page renders correctly otherwise.

Only 4 status filters visible ('À relancer', 'Actifs', 'Presque perdus', 'Perdus') + 'Tous'. The backend supports 5 statuses including 'en_retard' — there is no dedicated 'En retard' filter, so those clients only appear under 'Tous'.

issues: Recharts warnings about width(-1)/height(-1) at initial render — cosmetic console noise, charts do render after layout.

action_items: Verify Dashboard 'RDV À VENIR' KPI rendering — value shows '()' instead of the count. Check the widget template in /app/frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx (kpi-upcoming).
Add `data-testid="clients-status-page"` to the root element of /app/frontend/src/pages/ClientStatus.jsx.
Consider adding an 'En retard' filter tab to ClientStatus to match the 5 backend statuses.

Self-test not performed for AppointmentForm smart-slots card, ClientPhotos social-square/story buttons, and Dashboard 'Démarrer ma journée' CTA (needs today's RDV with geocoded client). Main agent should spot-check these flows in-browser.
critical_code_review_comments: Client Pydantic model (/app/backend/server.py) was missing lat/lng fields even though clients_create/update write them. Fixed.
server.py is ~1525 lines — getting large; consider splitting domains (clients, appointments, accounting, tour/slots) into separate routers.
mutable default `{}` used for `custom_fields` and `loyalty_counters` in Client model — safe in Pydantic v2 but flag as review item.
geocoding uses Nominatim without rate-limit backoff or caching TTL; acceptable given graceful fallback is in place.
/api/clients/status route ordering relies on being declared BEFORE /api/clients/{cid}. A passing regression test has been added (TestClientStatus.test_clients_status_returns_array_not_404).
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (added lat/lng Optional fields to Client model)
/app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (rewritten as full pytest suite covering 8 modules + regression)
test_credentials: None — auth disabled; backend returns hardcoded local-julien user.
seed_data_creation: Tests create `TEST_Tour_*` client + 1 appointment today then delete in TestZCleanup. Settings are restored to defaults after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green (pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py). For frontend, note: Settings route is /reglages (NOT /settings). All goal/brand testids exist at /reglages. Still to verify interactively: AppointmentForm smart-slots-card + suggest-slots-btn + slot-pick-0 flow; ClientPhotos social-square-{id}/social-story-{id} → social-preview-modal with social-dl-btn/social-share-btn; 'Démarrer ma journée' CTA appears only when an RDV exists today. Known minor UI issues to retest after fix: kpi-upcoming shows '()'; add clients-status-page testid; add 'En retard' filter tab.
rca of the issue: POST /api/clients returned 500 whenever address was supplied: Pydantic v2 `__setattr__` rejects assignment to undefined fields → ValueError. Reproduction: `curl -X POST .../api/clients -d '{"last_name":"X","address":"1 rue de la Paix"}'`. Mitigation: declared `lat: Optional[float] = None` / `lng: Optional[float] = None` in the Client BaseModel so auto_geocode results persist on the object. Validated via backend_test.py::TestSeedData::test_create_client (green).
